_scoring_notes.kinetic_formula: 40% intercept rate (scaled 90-100% → 0-100) + 20% absolute debris penalty (100 - debris*8) + 20% day-over-day trend (100 - delta*10) + 20% 7-day avg trend (100 - delta*10)
_scoring_notes.social_formula: Base 100. Deductions: -5 per NCEMA alert in last 24h, -5 US embassy partial advisory, -5 AUH partial restriction, -10 per full embassy departure advisory, -10 per airport closure

dailyBriefing: On March 20 (Eid al-Fitr), UAE air defences successfully intercepted all incoming Iranian ballistic missiles and drones in early-morning waves, with no injuries reported in Dubai. Debris from interceptions caused a limited fire at Jebel Ali Port (contained by 07:40) and struck the facade of a building near the DIFC/Business Bay corridor; Abu Dhabi's Habshan gas facilities were shut down after debris from intercepted missiles caused incidents at the site. The UAE government's official stance remains normal life — residents were advised to resume activities after each resolved alert. No embassy departure advisories issued. Supermarket shelves remain stocked though major chains are air-freighting produce to compensate for Hormuz disruptions; fuel stations fully operational.

schoolClosures.label: Distance Learning Only
schoolClosures.guidance: All schools remain closed for in-person attendance through March 31. KHDA-regulated Dubai schools plan to reopen April 6. Online classes continue as normal.

supermarketStock.label: Adequate — Air Freight Supplement
supermarketStock.guidance: Major chains (Carrefour, Lulu, Spinneys) remain stocked. Lulu has air-freighted 160+ tons of produce this month to offset Hormuz shipping disruptions. Prices rising on some imported goods.

fuelAvailability.label: Normal Supply
fuelAvailability.guidance: ENOC and ADNOC stations fully operational across Dubai. No queues or shortages reported. Monitor for price adjustments as global oil prices remain elevated.
